sound_type: cow call -- sharp alarm bark (300-800 Hz) and plaintive maternal mew (400-1,200 Hz). Two-vocalization repertoire. Year-round. E=5
ecosystem: Dense forest, river valleys, meadow edges. Year-round resident. Maternal communication peaks May-June calving.
season: Year-round. Bark: any threat season. Mew: peaks during calving (May-June) and cow-calf separation.

coordinates.description: Roosevelt Elk on the Olympic Peninsula and Cascades. Cow herds in river valleys and meadows.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Roosevelt Elk entry. Fourth Cervidae. Elk trio middle (197 bugle E=7, 198 cow call E=5, 199 rut roar E=7).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Cervidae: Deer Fawn (195, E=3), Deer Buck (196, E=4), Elk Bugle (197, E=7), Elk Cow Call (198, E=5).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Same as 197. Cervus (Latin deer), canadensis (Canada), roosevelti (Theodore Roosevelt).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Cervidae: fourth entry. Elk trio middle.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Cow elk: 200-300 kg. Smaller than bulls.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Bark: 0.2-0.5 sec, repeated 2-5 times. Mew: 0.5-2.0 sec.
song_metrics.note_structure: Two distinct vocalizations: explosive bark (alarm) and drawn-out mew (contact/maternal).
song_metrics.note_types: Bark (alarm, sharp, both sexes), mew (contact, nasal, primarily cows).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Bark: rapid repetitions during alarm. Mew: single or repeated calls during cow-calf contact.
song_metrics.timbre: Bark: sharp, explosive, percussive. Mew: nasal, plaintive, harmonically rich, individually recognizable.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Powerful and purposeful. Bark carries 500 m-1 km. Mew carries 200-500 m. Essential, not performative.
song_metrics.tempo: Bark: rapid bursts. Mew: deliberate, patient repetition.
song_metrics.context: Bark: predator/threat detection, year-round. Mew: cow-calf contact, calving season (May-Jun), herd cohesion.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Cow calls structurally similar across Roosevelt Elk populations. Individual variation (vocal fingerprint) is the key variable.
song_metrics.key_property: COW CALL. Bark + mew. Maternal. Individually recognizable. ELK TRIO MIDDLE. E=5. v1.49.339.

pelage.overall: Adult cow: brown body, lighter tan rump patch, no antlers. Slightly smaller than bull (200-300 kg). Less prominent neck mane.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: No antlers. Brown body. Tan rump. Smaller and more slender than bull. Often with calf nearby (May-October).
pelage.seasonal_variation: Summer: darker brown. Winter: slightly lighter. No antler cycle. Calves: spotted at birth, spots fade by fall.

physical_description.overall: Adult cow: 200-300 kg (440-660 lbs). 120-140 cm at shoulder. No antlers. Stocky. Tan rump patch.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Large brown ungulate without antlers. Tan rump. In herds. Often with calves (spring-summer).
physical_description.field_id: Large brown elk without antlers. In groups of 10-60. Listen for sharp bark (alarm) or nasal mew (cow-calf contact). Calving grounds in May-June. River valleys and meadow edges.

nodes.title: Cow-Calf Vocal Fingerprint
nodes.description: Each cow's mew is individually recognizable. Calf identifies mother within hours of birth. Vocal identity as biological necessity.

nodes.title: Bark Alarm -- Herd Alert
nodes.description: Sharp, explosive bark at 300-800 Hz. Carries 500 m-1 km. One bark triggers herd-wide response. Year-round vigilance.

nodes.title: Display vs. Connection
nodes.description: Bull: performance (bugle, roar, E=7). Cow: precision (bark, mew, E=5). The elk's vocal repertoire divided by function and sex.

nodes.title: Calving Season -- May-June
nodes.description: Cow separates from herd. Calf hidden 1-2 weeks. Mew for nursing contact. Most critical period for maternal vocalization.

cross_references.relationship: ELK TRIO! Bull bugle (E=7, display) vs. cow call (E=5, connection). Performance vs. precision.
ELK TRIO! Third entry completes the trio. Rut roar (E=7, combat). Bull's deep bellowing.
MATERNAL PARALLEL! Fawn bleat (E=3) and elk calf mew -- both infant contact calls. Same function.
QUIET COMMUNICATION! Both voices that matter for precision, not volume. The storyteller and the mother.
